channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Canadian ETF Market Expands with First Space Fund and New Offerings from Major Providers
item.description: The Canadian ETF marketplace saw significant growth in April with the introduction of 32 new funds. RBC Global Asset Management launched seven ETFs, including actively managed equity funds like RBC Ca...

item.title: US and Iran reportedly close to preliminary peace memorandum to end Gulf conflict
item.description: Iran is reviewing a new US proposal as both countries edge closer to a preliminary one-page memorandum aimed at ending the ongoing war in the Gulf. The proposal, reportedly consisting of 14 points, wo...

item.title: Simba beats JKT Tanzania 1-0 to stay close to Yanga in league race
item.description: Simba SC managed to keep their title hopes alive with a tight 1-0 win over JKT Tanzania at the Major General Isamuhyo Stadium on May 6, 2026. The only goal of the game came in the 53rd minute when exp...

item.title: Glasgow Museum Reports Missing Rodin Sculpture from Historic Collection
item.description: A museum in Glasgow has reported the disappearance of a plaster sculpture that was part of Auguste Rodin's famous ensemble, 'The Burghers of Calais.' This group of six male figures was created by Rodi...

item.title: Carolina Hurricanes Show Strong Playoff Form and Potential for Conference Final
item.description: The Carolina Hurricanes have started their second-round playoff series against the Philadelphia Flyers with two strong victories, echoing their earlier performance against the Ottawa Senators. After a...

item.title: Mom from Mpumalanga gives birth to quadruplets after expecting twins
item.description: Nomcebo Likhuleni, a 30-year-old woman from Buyelani, Mpumalanga, received an extraordinary surprise during her pregnancy. Initially expecting twins due to family history, she discovered at three mont...

item.title: US Proposal Gains Traction for Ending Iran Conflict and Restoring Strait of Hormuz Shipping
item.description: Tehran is currently considering a United States proposal aimed at ending the two-month conflict in the Middle East. The plan would allow for the resumption of safe and unhindered shipping through the ...

item.title: The Impact of GLP-1 Drugs on Bariatric Surgery Rates in the U.S.
item.description: Recent research indicates that the rise of GLP-1 medications, such as Ozempic and Wegovy, is correlated with a notable decline in bariatric surgeries in the United States. These drugs, which mimic the...

item.title: Major UK Supermarkets Criticize Aldi and Lidl Over Pricing Loophole
item.description: Three of the United Kingdom's largest supermarket chains have publicly criticized Aldi and Lidl for exploiting a pricing loophole that they claim undermines fair competition in the retail sector. The ...

item.title: Evan Rachel Wood Shares Abuse Allegations Against Marilyn Manson in New Documentary
item.description: HBO has released the trailer for the documentary 'Phoenix Rising,' which focuses on American actress and activist Evan Rachel Wood and her experiences with musician Marilyn Manson. The film, set to pr...

item.title: Alex Eala ready for her Rome clash with Frech
item.description: Filipino tennis sensation Alex Eala is in top form ahead of her match against Poland's Magdalena Fręch at the Internazionali BNL d’Italia in Rome. According to reports, Eala is both physically fit and...

item.title: BBNaija Season 11 Auditions To Begin Soon, Organisers Confirm
item.description: Big Brother Naija don officially announce say dem dey ready for Season 11. Organisers release teaser on their official X account on Tuesday, May 5, 2026, show say auditions go start soon, even though ...

item.title: Revilla’s Camp Awaits Court Decision on Bail for Malversation Case
item.description: The legal team of Senator Ramon “Bong” Revilla, Jr. is patiently awaiting the court's decision on his pending bail petition related to the P92.8 million flood control project in Pandi, Bulacan. Revill...

item.title: Princess of Wales visits children participating in brain research prior to royal tour
item.description: The Princess of Wales recently visited a group of children undergoing pioneering brain research, marking her first public engagement since completing cancer treatment. The visit was aimed at supportin...

item.title: Chalmers Outlines Budget Approach Amid Inflation Concerns
item.description: A week ahead of what he has called his most ambitious budget yet, Treasurer Jim Chalmers has defended his strategy against claims that high government spending is worsening inflation. Speaking across ...

item.title: Calgary police investigate fatal assault in Woodlands neighbourhood
item.description: Calgary police homicide investigators are examining the circumstances surrounding a fatal assault in the southwest Calgary community of Woodlands. Authorities were called to the 2100 block of Woodview...

item.title: Central African Republic: Foreign Aid Priorities Fail to Support Key Development Sectors
item.description: The Central African Republic (CAR) is among the world's least developed countries with very slow progress. Many people used to call it an 'aid orphan,' but researchers found that foreign aid has actua...

item.title: The Global Health and Environmental Impact of Plastic Pollution
item.description: Plastic pollution has become a pervasive global problem, with microplastics now detected in human blood, breast milk, heart arteries, lungs, testicles, brains, and placentas. These tiny particles, sma...

item.title: Family of Pune child rape-murder accused disowns him as outrage grows and SIT probe begins
item.description: A deeply disturbing incident in Pune has triggered widespread public outrage after a three-and-a-half-year-old girl was allegedly raped and murdered by a 65-year-old labourer, Bhimrao Kamble, on May 1...

item.title: Cybercriminal Forums Frustrated by Low-Quality AI Content
item.description: Cybercriminals and low-level hackers are increasingly frustrated by the presence of AI-generated content on forums where they discuss illegal activities. A study analyzing nearly 98,000 AI-related pos...
